rationale: The citation to Kumar and Pansari (2016) is used in a specific conceptual claim, not just a background list: the paper argues that higher engagement does not automatically create firm value unless it translates into value-adding actions. That claim is central to the citing paper's framework, which separates game engagement from value-added engagement and then models how one translates into the other. Even though the cited work is referenced briefly, it meaningfully informs the citing paper's core framing of the engagement-value link.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Kumar and Pansari (2016) as conceptual grounding for its key premise that engagement must convert into value-adding behaviors to generate business value. It then builds its gamification framework around this premise by distinguishing game engagement from value-added engagement outcomes.
